questions.question: What does your current fraud detection look like — rules-based or machine learning?
questions.intent: Establishes baseline maturity. Most PH banks are still rules-based.
questions.followUp: How often do you update those rules?

questions.question: Where are you in your BSP Circular 1213 compliance journey — have you started the FMS build-out?
questions.intent: Identifies urgency and budget timeline. June 2026 deadline is driving most PH bank deals.
questions.followUp: What's your target go-live date for the fraud management system?

questions.question: How many data sources feed your current transaction monitoring — and how real-time is that pipeline?
questions.intent: Assesses data integration complexity. Real-time scoring needs strong data architecture.
questions.followUp: Are those batch or streaming feeds?

questions.question: Do you have a formal model inventory — how many models are in production and who owns them?
questions.intent: Opens model governance conversation. Most PH banks lack centralized model registries.
questions.followUp: How do you track model performance drift?

questions.question: What's your current false positive rate on fraud alerts — and how does your ops team handle the volume?
questions.intent: Quantifies pain. High false positives = analyst fatigue = missed real fraud.
questions.followUp: How many analysts review alerts daily?

questions.question: Are you using network analytics to detect organized fraud rings, or is it still account-level detection?
questions.intent: Differentiator for SAS. Network/graph analytics finds syndicate fraud that rules miss.
questions.followUp: Have you seen cases of coordinated fraud across accounts?

questions.question: How does your AML team handle suspicious activity reporting today — manual or automated workflows?
questions.intent: AML is closely tied to fraud. Joint platform = efficiency. Maps to AMLC requirements.
questions.followUp: How long does a SAR take from detection to filing?

questions.question: Can your business users — compliance officers, not data scientists — adjust detection rules without IT involvement?
questions.intent: Business user access is a major SAS differentiator vs. code-only platforms.
questions.followUp: How long does a rule change take end-to-end today?

questions.question: When you deploy a new model to production, what does that process look like — and how long does it take?
questions.intent: Model deployment pain is universal. SAS Model Manager automates this pipeline.
questions.followUp: Is there a formal approval workflow before a model goes live?

questions.question: Are you evaluating GenAI or LLMs for any fraud/compliance use cases — even experimentally?
questions.intent: Opens GenAI conversation. SAS has responsible AI governance built in.
questions.followUp: What guardrails are you considering for GenAI in regulated processes?

questions.question: How do you handle real-time scoring at the point of transaction — sub-second latency requirement?
questions.intent: Real-time is table stakes for card fraud. Tests if they have the infra for ML at scale.
questions.followUp: What's your acceptable latency for a fraud decision?

questions.question: If BSP asks you to demonstrate how a specific fraud decision was made, can you produce that audit trail today?
questions.intent: Explainability is a regulatory requirement. SAS provides full decision lineage.
questions.followUp: How do you currently document model decisions for regulators?
